g.data-narration: This diagram explains UML models that supplement use case diagrams for comprehensive system modeling.
g.text: UML Models That Supplement the Use Case
Bridging the Gap Between Requirements and Implementation

g.data-narration: Use case diagrams show what the system does but not how internally. Supplemental UML models bridge this gap.
g.text: Use case diagrams answer "WHAT" the system does from user's perspective
but they don't show HOW the system achieves these goals internally.
Supplemental UML models fill this gap with detailed behavioral and structural views.

g.data-narration: Five key UML diagrams supplement use cases: Activity, Sequence, State Machine, Collaboration, and Class diagrams.
Activity diagrams use start/end circles, rounded activity boxes, diamond decisions, and swimlanes for workflow modeling.
This activity diagram shows a fund transfer workflow with login, validation, processing, and confirmation steps.
Sequence diagrams show object interactions over time with lifelines, activation bars, and various message types.
This sequence diagram shows library book issue process with numbered messages between member, librarian, and database.
g.text: Sequence Diagram Example: Library Issue Book

g.data-narration: State machine diagrams model object states with transitions triggered by events and guarded by conditions.
This state diagram shows ATM cash withdrawal from idle through card insert, PIN entry, menu, withdrawal, dispensing, and completion.
g.text: State Diagram Example: ATM Cash Withdrawal

g.data-narration: Collaboration diagrams show object relationships with numbered messages, emphasizing structural connections over time ordering.
Class diagrams show static structure with classes, attributes, methods, and relationships like association and inheritance.
Choose activity diagrams for workflows, sequence for time ordering, state for state changes, collaboration for structure, and class for static relationships.
